name: Richard Richter
title: DevOps and AI Consultant
summary: DevOps Engineer and Scrum Master with experience in cloud migration, process automation, and agile team leadership in international remote teams.

tasks: Built and optimized CI/CD pipelines and Infrastructure-as-Code solutions for clients in banking, insurance, and energy
Tool migrations (e.g. Zephyr → Xray, Opsgenie → JSM Operations) seamlessly integrated into Atlassian ecosystems
Banking project: Led the transition from Scrum to Kanban – reduced meeting effort and attendance, saving approx. €500,000 per year
Insurance project: Tool migration with approx. 40 % reduction in annual license costs and faster workflows through direct Atlassian integration
Led XALT’s internal Atlassian on-premise-to-cloud migration: reduced operational overhead and maintenance effort
Research and development of local AI solutions with llama.cpp and harnesses such as Hermes and OpenClaw – enabling AI use in privacy-sensitive and restrictive environments without cloud dependency
Facilitated core agile events (dailies, 1:1s, retrospectives) for a fully Europe-distributed team
Workshops to identify bottlenecks and process visualization with BPMN 2.0 – standardized workflows and increased efficiency
Applied the 3M philosophy from the Toyota Production System (Muda, Mura, Muri): reduced waste, balanced workload, and stabilized processes
Supported deployment requests with focus on resource right-sizing – optimized costs and stabilized performance
Scaled applications across a multi-cluster setup (Alpha, Dev, Stage, Prod) with Rancher and ArgoCD
Shifted deployment strategy from trunk-based to GitOps: reduced redundancy and separated changes more clearly
Cloud cost optimization through better storage options, cleanup of unused resources, and consistent right-sizing
Supported cloud migration from Azure to AWS: completed migration in 9 instead of 12 months while maintaining stability of existing Azure infrastructure
Supported the release of a low-code solution based on resolve.io
Server hardening and expansion of monitoring with Prometheus/Grafana for improved operational reliability
In-house workshops on programming logic and data structures for operators – improved collaboration with developers and reduced silos
Built and led a student development team (3 people) with agile Kanban planning
Greenfield development of a web app to automate network processes: ruleset exports, certificate renewals, and change requests
CI/CD from GitHub to Kubernetes: fully automated deployments and reduction of manual processes from days to seconds
Solution remained in productive use for at least three years after graduation
German-as-a-foreign-language coaching: learners reached B1 level in one third of the regular course duration
IT training: manual processes that previously took days reduced to seconds through automation
Practical individual and group lessons in German, mathematics, and computer science, tailored to professional challenges

items.thesis: Facilitating agile work culture through the automation of computer network related processes

items.role: International Youth Volunteer Service via DRK in Haifa, Israel
tasks: Taught film theory: independently prepared lessons and learning materials
Managed professional film and audio recording equipment: organized loans to students and regularly checked inventory
Technical support for audio and video equipment during institute events
Assisted with booking and management of the affiliated guesthouse
Rebuilt the website for institute and guesthouse on WordPress: improved information findability and simplified booking process
